# Evidence Narrative Fixture

## Observation

Conference presentations often alternate between a claim slide and a proof slide. The proof slide may contain a chart, a short table, and a sentence that explains the takeaway. The layout should keep those objects together because the presenter needs the audience to connect the visual evidence to the spoken argument.

```ranked-bars
Readability, 88
Template preservation, 92
Image ratio handling, 90
Content splitting, 86
```

## Takeaway

The result shows that the strongest improvement is preserving the existing template while adding smarter content separation. The second improvement is avoiding unnecessary images when the source material does not justify them.

| Finding | Impact |
| --- | --- |
| Template master remains authoritative | Existing HCS theme is not distorted |
| Dense prose uses hierarchy | Long slides stay usable |
| Image fit preserves aspect ratio | Screenshots and figures avoid stretching |
